description: Calculate the estimated baseline parking demand for every zone based on residential and commercial building stock using the 'buildings' and 'parcels' tables from /home/ga/urbansim_projects/data/sanfran_public.h5. In a notebook at /home/ga/urbansim_projects/notebooks/parking_demand_analysis.ipynb using the UrbanSim (Python 3) kernel, determine parking demand using 1.2 spaces per residential unit and 2.5 spaces per 1,000 square feet of non-residential area, treating missing values as 0. Aggregate total residential, non-residential, and combined parking demand by 'zone_id'. Calculate 'spaces_per_acre' for each zone, ensuring total parcel acres are aggregated directly from the parcels table to avoid double-counting land area. Export the zone-level results sorted by total spaces in descending order to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/zone_parking_demand.csv. Generate a stacked bar chart of the Top 15 zones by total spaces (x-axis: zone_id, y-axis: spaces, stacked by residential vs. non-residential) titled "Top 15 Zones by Estimated Parking Demand" and save it to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/top_parking_zones.png. Save a citywide summary to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/citywide_parking_summary.json with keys: 'citywide_residential_spaces', 'citywide_non_residential_spaces', and 'citywide_total_spaces'.
